title: Proclamation No. 572, s. 1940
text:
MALACAÑAN PALACE MANILA

PROCLAMATION NO. 572

RESERVING FOR SCHOOL PURPOSES FIVE PARCELS OF THE PUBLIC DOMAIN SITUATED IN THE BARRIOS OF CARPENTO, BATONAN, TIDMAN, BACULIN AND SAN JUAN, MUNICIPALITY OF HINATUAN, PROVINCE OF SURIGAO, ISLAND OF MINDANAO.

Upon the recommendation of the Secretary of Agriculture and Commerce and pursuant to the provisions of section eighty-three of Commonwealth Act Numbered One hundred and forty-one, as amended, I hereby withdraw from sale or settlement and reserve for school purposes, under the administration of the Director of Education, subject to private rights, if any there be, the following parcels of the public domain, situated in the barrios of Carpenito, Batonan, Tidman, Baculin and San Juan, municipality of Hinatuan, province of Surigao, island of Mindanao, and particularly described in Bureau of Lands plan Mr-421, to wit:

Lot No. 1, Mr-421 (School Site —Barrio of Carpenito, municipality of Hinatuan). —Beginning at a point marked 1 on Bureau of Lands plan Mr-421, N. 28° 02′ W. 122.94 m. from B. L. B. M. No. 1, barrio of Carpenito, municipality of Hinatuan, thence N. 5° 53′ E. 96.76 m. to point 2; S. 82° 21′ E. 138.42 m. to point 3; S. 3° 18′ W. 88.66 m. to point 4; N. 85° 42′ W. 142.41 m. to point 1, point of beginning.

Containing an area of 13,006 square meters.

All corners are B. L. concrete monuments.

Bounded on the north by property of Ramon Camba; on the east, by property of Andrea Luna de Fraga; on the south, by barrio street; and on the west, by proposed barrio street.

Bearings true. Declination 1° 30′ E.

Points referred to are marked on Bureau of Lands plan Mr-421, sheet No. 1.

Surveyed: August 8, 1939. Approved: April 29, 1940.

Lot No. 2, Mr-421 (School Site — Barrio of Batonan, municipality of Hinatuan). —Beginning at a point marked 1 on Bureau of Lands plan Mr-421, S. 3° 64′ E. 9,276.20 m., more or less, from B. L. B. M. No. 2, barrio of Barobo, municipality of Lianga, thence S. 81° 21′ E. 73.26 m. to point 2; S. 48° 34′ E. 14.53 m. to point 3; S. 15° 55′ W. 110.37 m. to point 4; N. 70° 04′ W . 84.46 m. to point 6; N. 15° 13′ E. 72.81 m. to point 6; N. 14° 39′ E. 28.63 m. to point 1, point of beginning.

Containing an area of 9,280 square meters.

Points 1, 3, 4 and 5, B. L. concrete monuments; and points 2 and 6, “X” cuts on trees.

Bounded on the northeast by bank protection of Hinatuan River; on the southeast and southwest, by property of Potenciana Vda. de Potenciando; and on the northwest, by property of Potenciana Vda. de Potenciando.

Bearings true. Declination 1° 30′ E.

Points referred to are marked on Bureau of Lands plan Mr-421, sheet No. 2.

Surveyed: August 10-12, 1939. Approved: April 29, 1940.

Lot No. 3, Mr-421 (School Site — Barrio of Tidman, municipality of Hinatuan). —Beginning at a point marked 1 on Bureau of Lands plan Mr-421, S. 58° 48′ W. 99.98 m. from B. L. B. M. No. 1, Barrio of Tidman, municipality of Hinatuan, thence N. 20° 19′ E. 99.38 m. to point 2; S. 64° 41′ E. 13.31 m. to point 3; S. 71° 22′ E. 19.27 m. to point 4; S. 65° 57′ E. 14.55 m. to point 5; S. 68° 52′ E. 12.51 m. to point 6; S. 65° 41′ E. 12.17 m. to point 7; S. 71° 34′ E. 57.97 m. to point 8; S. 23° 48′ W. 58.68 m. to point 9; N. 71° 41′ W. 55.91 m. to point 10; S. 26° 47′ W. 37.60 m. to point 11; N. 70° 24′ W. 66.97 m. to point 1, point of beginning.

Containing an area of 10,157 square meters.

Points 3, 4 and 6, “X” cuts on trees, and the rest are B. L. concrete monuments.

Bounded on the northeast by properties of Pedro Salilin, Carlos Ravelo, Severino Piano, Saturnino Epi and Severino Agurto and public land; on the southeast and southwest, by public land; and on the northwest, by barrio street.

Bearings true. Declination 1° 30′ E.

Points referred to are marked on Bureau of Lands plan Mr-421, sheet No. 3.

Surveyed: August 2, 1939.

Approved: April 29, 1940.

Lot No. 4, Mr-421 (School Site —Barrio of Baculin, municipality of Hinatuan). —Beginning at a point marked 1 on Bureau of Lands plan Mr-421, N. 32° 38′ W. 6,876.50 m, more or less, from B. L. B. M. No. 1 Barrio of Port Lamon, municipality of Hinatuan, thence S. 69° 43′ W. 134.05 m. to point 2; North 96.95 m. to point 3; N. 3° 44′ W. 12.14 m. to point 4; N. 74° 46′ E. 93.01 m. to point 5; S. 22° 55′ E. 94.47 m. to point 1, point of beginning.

Containing an area of 11,190 square meters.

Points 1, 2 and 5, B. L. concrete monuments; and points 3 and 4, “X” cuts on trees.

Bounded on the northeast by barrio street; on the southeast and west, by public land; and on the northwest, by barrio street.

Bearings true. Declination 1° 30′ E.

Points referred to are marked on Bureau of Lands plan Mr-421, sheet No. 4.

Surveyed: July 27-28, 1939.

Approved: April 29, 1940.

Lot No. 5, Mr-421 (School Site —Barrio of San Juan municipality of Hinatuan).— Beginning at a point marked 1 on Bureau of Lands plan Mr-421, N. 11° 66′ E 76.59 m. from B. L. B. M. No. 2, barrio of San Juan, municipality of Hinatuan, thence N. 7° 46′ E. 37.78 m. to point 2 ; S. 80° 32’ E. 46.84 m. to point 3; S. 9° 31′ W. 33.95 m. to point 4 ; S. 1° W. 77.78 m. to point 5; N. 88° 08′ W. 35.26 m. to point 6; N. 9° 19′ E. 77.43 m. to point 7; N. 79° 12′ W. 20.72 m. to point 1, point of beginning.

Containing an area of 4,049 square meters.

Point 1, B. L. B. M. No. 1, points 2 and 5, B. L. concrete monuments; points 3 and 4, “X” cuts on trees; and points 6 and 7, old corners.

Bounded on the north by barrio street; on the east, by seashore; on the south, by barrio street and property of the municipal government of Hinatuan; and on the west, by property of the municipal government of Hinatuan and barrio street.

Bearings true. Declination 1° 30′ E.

Points referred to are marked on Bureau of Lands plan Mr-421, sheet No. 5.

Surveyed: August 4, 1939.

Approved: April 29, 1940.

In witness whereof, I have hereunto set my hand and caused the seal of the Commonwealth of the Philippines to be affixed.

Done at the City of Manila, this eighth day of July, in the year of Our Lord, nineteen hundred and forty, and of the Commonwealth of the Philippines, the fifth.

MANUEL L. QUEZON President of the Philippines

By the President: JORGE B. VARGAS Secretary to the President

Source: Presidential Museum and Library
